Based on the provided codebase and documentation, update the project documentation to reflect recent code changes while adhering to these requirements:

1. SCOPE & COVERAGE
- Review and update documentation for all modified code in the latest release
- Document new features, functions, APIs, and configuration options
- Update examples and code snippets to demonstrate current functionality
- Identify and document any deprecated or removed features

2. REQUIRED UPDATES
- Function signatures, parameters, and return values
- Usage examples with expected inputs/outputs
- Environment setup and prerequisites 
- Breaking changes and migration guides
- Known limitations and workarounds
- API endpoint specifications (if applicable)

3. DOCUMENTATION STANDARDS
- Follow existing documentation style guide
- Use clear, concise technical writing
- Include inline code examples
- Add version numbers where relevant
- Maintain consistent formatting
- Validate all links and references

4. REVIEW PROCESS
- Submit documentation changes via pull request
- Include references to relevant code commits
- Request technical review from feature owners
- Address reviewer feedback before merging
- Verify accuracy of all technical content

5. DELIVERABLES
- Updated API reference documentation
- Revised developer guides and tutorials
- Migration guide for breaking changes
- Changelog entries
- README updates

Flag any sections requiring additional input from the development team for clarification.
Be mindful of the formatting and style guidelines established in the documentation.
Keep the frontmatter headers, if they exist.
